entry.title: Joule Studio: Why is build_default required if standard plan exists?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m currently setting up Joule Studio in SAP BTP and reviewing the prerequisites.

According to the documentation, Joule Studio requires an entitlement for SAP Build Process Automation with the service plan “build_default”.

However, in my subaccount I already have the “standard” (standard-application) plan assigned, which provides full production capabilities for SAP Build Process Automation (workflows, RPA, monitoring, etc.).

From my understanding:

  • The standard plan offers full functionality for productive use

  • The build_default plan seems to provide a more limited or base-level feature set

This raises a concern, as introducing or switching to build_default could potentially limit existing capabilities.

My questions are:

  1. Is the build_default plan technically required for Joule Studio, even if the standard plan is already available?

  2. Is there a dependency or integration requirement that specifically relies on build_default?

  3. Can both plans coexist without impacting existing Build Process Automation scenarios?

  4. What is the recommended entitlement setup for using Joule Studio in a productive BTP environment?

Any clarification or best practices would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: How to set up combine Multiple Sales Schedule Agreement as one Delivery Order
entry.content:
1. I create 2 Sales Schedule Agreements with the same Customer, Material and Schedule Line Date

2. When I use app : Create Outbound Delivery with Order Reference 

I can combine multiple Sales Schedule Agreements into one Delivery Order 

3. But when I use app : Sales Order Schedule Lines

I can't combine multiple Sales Schedule Agreements into one Delivery Order and Customer is already flagged for the "Order Combination" in the Customer Master Data

Could you please help or suggest how to set up a combined Multiple Sales Schedule Agreement as one Delivery Order?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to set up combine Multiple Sales Schedule Agreeme...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Hope you are doing great.

We have identified that the behavior is related to how the app “Sales Order Schedule Lines – Due for Delivery” processes delivery creation depending on the execution mode.

In this app, there are two different processing options:

1. Dialog (Create single delivery in dialog)

  • The system processes each selected document individually
  • As a result, only one outbound delivery per reference document is created
  • Combination of multiple Sales Scheduling Agreements is not supported in this mode

2. Background

  • The system processes documents via backend logic (function module RV_DELIVERY_CREATE)
  • In this case, multiple documents can be passed together
  • Therefore, order combination is supported, and a single outbound delivery can be created

This is standard system behavior:

  • Dialog -> no combination (one delivery per document)
  • Background -> combination possible

If your business scenario requires combining multiple Sales Scheduling Agreements into one delivery, we recommend using:

  • The Background option in Sales Order Schedule Lines – Due for Delivery, or
  • The app “Create Outbound Delivery with Order Reference”, which supports combination by design

Hope the above clarifies your queries.

Best regards,
Letícia

entry.title: Re: Document Migration: Understanding User Difference Between Onboarding 1.0 and BizX
entry.content:
Dear @Sue-Lhymn 

The bulk download sends a zip file with no identification on file name itself.

Each zip file will have the documents and a index file. So you will have to check the CSV index file to identify the files.

You need to start / test a bulk download to see information as there is no pre-report.

Best regards,
Jacson
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: XML for Adobe Master template
entry.content:
A work around will be to put a break point within the CL_SOMU_FORM_SERVICES class.

That has to be done through the ADT tool. "Eclipse"

Within the method "get_document" put a breakpoint after  "__get_data( ".

Execute the output, when the debugger will open, select the variable ev_xml. It will contain the XML including the master form template section.

Make sure to remove the limitation of the variable display when the breakpoint stop.

Right click on the variable, select Show Detail as -> Default View

Right click in the view box, select "Max Length" and set it to "0" => illimited

Right click again on the variable "ev_xml", select "Show Detail as" -> XML view

Now you have the full XML including the Form master data.

entry.title: Re: EWM – STO using same HU number
entry.content:
Hi @krisli1 ,

Even in Warehouse 1000, you can still receive the HU using an internal number range, but the HU number must stay within the defined range threshold.

Best Regards,

Yuri Taranto
SAP EWM Consultant
